legend.hypergraph: Each translucent shape is one coordinated Liverpool attacking phase.
legend.graph: Pairwise links only show the pass-to-pass skeleton of the same move.

chapters.title: Match Overview
chapters.summary: Liverpool's strongest attacking moves usually involve four to six players before the shot. In hypergraph mode, those group actions stay visible as one coordinated relation instead of being flattened into isolated pass links.

chapters.title: Firmino Opener
chapters.summary: The opening goal emerges from a right-side overload: Gomez steps up, Salah keeps the ball alive, Can recycles the attack, and Firmino finishes the cross.

annotations.title: Gomez supplies the width
annotations.body: Liverpool keep the overload alive through a repeat wide involvement before the final delivery.

annotations.title: Firmino attacks the final gap
annotations.body: The finish completes a four-player action that is clearer as one coordinated hyperedge than as isolated pass links.

chapters.title: Mane Through the Left Half-Space
chapters.summary: Liverpool move the ball through Gomez, Wijnaldum, Can, and Firmino before Mane attacks the inside-left lane. This is a clean five-player hyperedge.

annotations.title: The move starts deep and climbs in layers
annotations.body: Liverpool advance through successive supporting touches before Mane receives the final attacking advantage.

annotations.title: Mane attacks the inside-left channel
annotations.body: This chapter works well as a higher-order relation because the danger comes from the group shape, not only the final shot.

chapters.title: Salah in Transition
chapters.summary: This move is intentionally sparse. Hypergraph mode collapses almost to a graph, showing how a direct transition differs from Liverpool's multi-player buildup patterns.

annotations.title: Transition becomes almost pairwise
annotations.body: This phase is intentionally sparse, which makes it useful for contrasting graph and hypergraph views.

chapters.title: Late Cross, Late Finish
chapters.summary: The fourth goal combines Firmino's layoff, Can's acceleration, Salah's cross, and Sturridge's finish. It is a textbook higher-order action group built from multiple roles.

annotations.title: Salah creates the final delivery
annotations.body: The cross is only one step in a multi-role sequence that spans buildup, burst, delivery, and finish.

annotations.title: Sturridge finishes the hyperedge
annotations.body: The final shot closes a dense, high-order action that feels more complete as one group relation.

chapters.title: Free Explore
chapters.summary: Switch filters, compare graph versus hypergraph views, and inspect how the player group changes from one Liverpool attacking phase to the next.
